metadata.title: Web Research Profile Overrides - Batch 8K
metadata.notes: Public-web research only. Ownership is stated at the cultivar or right level; later employers and program successors are not treated as owners of earlier rights without supporting registry, patent, or assignment evidence. Personal residential details are intentionally omitted.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Hungarian official variety records identify Andor Szabó as a co-breeder with fractional breeder shares, while the applicant/maintainer for the cited Syrah and Piros Bakator records is the University of Pécs Viticulture and Oenology Research Institute. The records do not establish personal ownership of those rights.
profiles.leadershipSummary: A 2014 Croatian agriculture-ministry report identifies Szabó as a technical technician visiting from the Pécs Viticulture and Oenology Institute. The program continues institutionally at the University of Pécs; no current role, personal commercial operation, or individual succession plan was verified for Szabó.
profiles.primaryContactName: University of Pécs Viticulture and Oenology Research Institute

webResearchSources.label: NÉBIH official variety committee record naming breeder shares and the Pécs applicant/maintainer
Croatian Ministry of Agriculture report identifying Szabó's Pécs institute role in 2014
University of Pécs institutional contact page

profiles.webResearchNotes: Identity normalized to Andor Szabó from the unaccented source name. The evidence supports a historical public-institute breeder/technician, not a separately actionable private owner.

profiles.ownershipSummary: NARO publications identify Shigeki Moriya as a breeder in its apple program, and NARO is the institutional developer/rightsholder for the cited cultivars. His later move to the Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries Research Council does not transfer historical NARO cultivar rights to that council or to Moriya personally.
profiles.headquarters: Tokyo, Japan (current council affiliation); Morioka, Iwate, Japan (historical NARO apple program)
profiles.leadershipSummary: J-GLOBAL records a career at predecessor/NARO fruit-tree institutes from 2004 through March 2024 and an Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries Research Council affiliation from April 2024. Cultivar stewardship and licensing remain institutional at NARO; no personal commercial entity or individual successor was found.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Institutional licensing contact for historical NARO rights
profiles.candidateParent: Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries Research Council
profiles.candidateParentBasis: J-GLOBAL lists this affiliation from April 2024, but the public profile does not supply a current job title and the affiliation is not evidence of title to NARO-bred cultivars.

webResearchSources.label: J-GLOBAL researcher profile and employer timeline
NARO Research Report for the apple cultivar Kinshu
NARO official licensing page

profiles.webResearchNotes: Record split required between Moriya's historical NARO breeding work and his post-April-2024 council affiliation. Neither the later employer nor the individual should inherit NARO ownership in the dashboard.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Plant-patent records name Alan C. Tenscher as a co-inventor in the UC Davis grape program and record assignment to The Regents of the University of California. The cited rights are institutional, not personal, and a 2022 UC Davis event listing him as a Napa consultant must remain separate from his current UC Davis employment record.
profiles.headquarters: Davis, California, United States (current UC Davis role); Napa, California, United States (consultant listing in 2022)
profiles.leadershipSummary: UC Davis currently lists Tenscher as SRA 1 in Viticulture and Enology. The patented breeding team included Andrew Walker, Summaira Riaz, and Ninfa Romero; UC Davis provides institutional continuity after Walker's retirement, rather than a personally owned Tenscher program.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: SRA 1, Viticulture and Enology, UC Davis

webResearchSources.label: UC Davis current staff directory
USPTO plant patent record for Paseante Noir, including Regents assignment
UC Davis 2022 Andrew Walker event listing Tenscher as a consultant

profiles.webResearchNotes: The exact person is well resolved, but the aggregate record crosses a documented consultant period and current public-university employment. Cultivar rights should be attached to the Regents patent/assignment records, not to Tenscher or the consultant listing.

profiles.ownershipSummary: MAFF's official registration for the citrus cultivar ASUMI names Naoko Nakajima among the breeders and NARO as the holder. NARO publications also place her in institutional fruit research. No evidence supports personal ownership or a private breeding business.
profiles.leadershipSummary: The available record is that of a NARO research contributor within a multi-scientist citrus and fruit program. Current personal title was not verified; cultivar maintenance, licensing, and bench continuity are handled by NARO rather than by an individual successor.
profiles.candidateParent: National Agriculture and Food Research Organization (NARO)
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Institutional author affiliations and MAFF's holder record support NARO affiliation, but no current staff title was located for Naoko Nakajima.

webResearchSources.label: MAFF official registration for citrus cultivar ASUMI
NARO research report for the citrus cultivar Aurastar
Research article carrying Nakajima's NARO Tsukuba affiliation

profiles.webResearchNotes: Resolved as Naoko Nakajima, not the separately published NARO grape breeder Ikuko Nakajima. The current job title remains unverified, so the institutional link is retained as candidateParent.

profiles.ownershipSummary: MAFF records show Shigeru Kawahito as a breeder and one of three individual holders for Awa-ko and related family-bred strawberries; later records use different co-holder groups. A Chinese official PBR record for MIB001 instead identifies Inoue Lime Industry Co. as owner while naming the Kawahito breeders. Ownership must therefore be split by cultivar and jurisdiction rather than assigned wholesale to Kawahito or to a family grouping.
profiles.headquarters: Tokushima Prefecture, Japan (breeding activity; no formal business headquarters verified)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Repeated official records show a family breeding bench including Shinichi and Kenichi Kawahito, with Aiko and Mikiko Kawahito appearing on a later cultivar. No incorporated Kawahito breeding entity, formal leadership titles, or succession plan was verified.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Multiple official PBR records repeatedly name the same family co-breeders/co-holders, but no legal entity or formal titles were found.

webResearchSources.label: MAFF official registration for Awa-ko
MAFF official registration bulletin for Kamui Red
China Ministry of Agriculture official PBR grant list naming MIB001 owner and breeders

profiles.webResearchNotes: Residential addresses published in PBR records were deliberately excluded. No public business email or telephone was verified. Awa-ko's cited Japanese right expired in 2024; that status must not be generalized to the other cultivars.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The Hort16A plant patent names Hinga D. Marsh as a co-inventor and records Zespri Group Limited as the current assignee. Historical breeding work occurred in the HortResearch program, which later continued within Plant & Food Research. Neither that institutional succession nor Marsh's employment establishes a transfer of Zespri-assigned rights back to the researcher or later employer.
profiles.headquarters: Te Puke, New Zealand (documented kiwifruit research site); Auckland, New Zealand (Plant & Food Research head office)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Marsh worked in a kiwifruit breeding bench with Russell Lowe and Mark McNeilage, and later records include Elizabeth Popowski. Plant & Food Research provides institutional program continuity; the public sources reviewed do not establish Marsh's current role or a named personal successor.
profiles.candidateParent: The New Zealand Institute for Plant and Food Research Limited
profiles.candidateParentBasis: A 2013 scientific publication gives a Plant & Food Research, Te Puke affiliation for Hinga Marsh, but it does not prove current employment or ownership of Zespri-assigned patents.

webResearchSources.label: Hort16A US plant patent and assignment record
2013 publication carrying Hinga Marsh's Plant & Food Research, Te Puke affiliation
Plant & Food Research official locations and Te Puke site

profiles.webResearchNotes: The 2013 publication renders the middle initial as E., while patent records use D.; the uncommon full name, crop, institution, and Te Puke program strongly align, but the initial discrepancy is retained as a caution. Rights remain split by patent/PBR assignee. Plant & Food Research is now a group of the Bioeconomy Science Institute, which is not treated as retroactive title evidence.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Official PBR material for CEBASRED names Antonio Molina Gómez and Antonio Molina Gómez Jr. separately among the breeders and identifies Spain's CSIC as the rightsholder. The senior Antonio Molina Gómez is therefore a team breeder/agronomic contributor, not the personal owner of the CEBAS-CSIC portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: Campus Universitario de Espinardo, Murcia 30100, Spain
profiles.leadershipSummary: CEBAS-CSIC's current fruit-breeding materials list Antonio Molina in agronomic management within a larger team led through the public research center. The bench includes David Ruiz, Federico Dicenta, Manuel Rubio, José Egea, María Dolores Nortes, and Antonio Molina Gómez Jr.; institutional continuity sits with CEBAS-CSIC.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: Current program material supports an agronomic-management affiliation, but no independent formal employment title for the senior Antonio Molina Gómez was located.

webResearchSources.label: Official Turkish PBR bulletin for CEBASRED naming both senior and junior breeders and CSIC as owner
CEBAS-CSIC fruit breeding and plant-variety management program document
CSIC official CEBAS center profile

profiles.webResearchNotes: Keep the exact senior record separate from Antonio Molina Gómez Jr. The owner shown for CEBASRED is CSIC; no personal ownership or public personal business contact was found.

profiles.ownershipSummary: The exact input resolves to Frederick G. Gmitter Jr. UF/IFAS identifies him as a citrus genetics and breeding professor. Representative plant-patent records list Florida Foundation Seed Producers, Inc. as applicant/assignee, so UF/FFSP licensing records govern the cultivars rather than personal title by Gmitter.
profiles.headquarters: Citrus Research and Education Center, Lake Alfred, Florida, United States
profiles.leadershipSummary: Gmitter is a current UF/IFAS professor in a continuing public citrus-breeding program. The institutional bench includes Jude Grosser, Manjul Dutt, and José Chaparro, and FFSP manages commercial variety licensing; the record is not a private founder-led business.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Professor, Citrus Genetics and Breeding, UF/IFAS

webResearchSources.label: UF/IFAS official directory profile
UF/IFAS official citrus breeding program page
US plant patent for citrus cultivar 914 naming FFSP as applicant/assignee

profiles.webResearchNotes: Canonical dataset spelling is retained, but the normalized identity is Frederick G. Gmitter Jr. The public-university role and FFSP licensing structure make this a verified non-actionable institutional profile.

profiles.ownershipSummary: J.E.L. Cripps resolves to John Ernest Lane Cripps, who spent his working career with Western Australia's Department of Agriculture. Western Australian government records describe state plant-variety rights and royalties for the Cripps Pink/Pink Lady and Cripps Red/Sundowner apples. APAL's official history says the state department developed the Pink Lady trademark and assigned the global mark to APAL's predecessor in 1998; cultivar rights and trademark ownership must not be conflated or assigned personally to Cripps.
profiles.headquarters: Western Australia, Australia (historical state breeding program); East Melbourne, Victoria, Australia (current APAL brand management)
profiles.leadershipSummary: Cripps was a state-employed breeder rather than a private rightsholder. Current continuity is divided between Western Australian government horticulture/IP functions for the historical cultivar program and Apple and Pear Australia Limited for Pink Lady brand management; no personal successor is asserted.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Pink Lady brand and media contact

webResearchSources.label: Western Australian Museum biographical record and department career
Western Australian government statement on state PVR and royalties
APAL official history of the Pink Lady trademark assignment

profiles.webResearchNotes: Use John Ernest Lane Cripps as the normalized identity. Historical cultivar/PVR economics belonged to the State of Western Australia; Pink Lady trademark ownership later moved to APAL's predecessor. APAL is a brand contact, not evidence that it owns every underlying cultivar right.

profiles.ownershipSummary: János Apostol was a Hungarian state-institute stone-fruit breeder. NÉBIH records identify cultivar-specific breeder shares and applicant/maintainer entities such as the Érd fruit-research public-benefit company. Current MATE pages document continuation of the Érd/Budatétény breeding portfolio, but they do not by themselves prove that every historical right or maintenance obligation transferred to MATE.
profiles.headquarters: Érd and Budatétény, Hungary (historical and successor fruit-breeding program)
profiles.leadershipSummary: The historical bench included Sándor Brózik and other state-program breeders. The work is now presented through the Hungarian University of Agriculture and Life Sciences horticultural institute and fruit-research center; current station leadership and the institutional team provide bench continuity, while Apostol's current personal role was not verified.
profiles.primaryContactName: MATE Horticultural Institute, Fruitgrowing Research Centre
profiles.candidateParent: MATE Horticultural Institute, Fruitgrowing Research Centre
profiles.candidateParentBasis: MATE presents the continuing cherry portfolio and institutional program, but no current personal title for Apostol or blanket transfer of each historical right was verified.

webResearchSources.label: Hungarian Intellectual Property Office award biography
NÉBIH official fruit-variety record naming breeder shares and applicant/maintainer
MATE official cherry cultivar portfolio and successor-program contact

profiles.webResearchNotes: Identity normalized to János Apostol. Record split is required across cultivar-specific maintainers and the sequence of Érd state/public-benefit and MATE institutional structures. Current MATE stewardship must not be projected backward as title to every historical right.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Mikio Shiraishi appears as a breeder on national-institute cultivars held by NARO and on later Fukuoka cultivars held by Fukuoka Prefecture. NARO rights such as Kishuu must remain with NARO, while Fukuoka rights such as Suzuka remain with the prefecture; his later Fukuoka employment is not a transfer of the national portfolio.
profiles.headquarters: Chikushino, Fukuoka, Japan (current prefectural research center); Akitsu, Hiroshima, Japan (historical national fruit-tree program)
profiles.leadershipSummary: J-GLOBAL lists Shiraishi as a specialist researcher at the Fukuoka Agriculture and Forestry Research Center. Both NARO and Fukuoka operate team-based public breeding programs, so continuity and licensing are institutional rather than personal.
profiles.primaryContactName: Fukuoka Agriculture and Forestry Research Center

webResearchSources.label: J-GLOBAL profile for Mikio Shiraishi at the Fukuoka research center
MAFF official registration for NARO-held Kishuu
MAFF official registration for Fukuoka-held Suzuka

profiles.webResearchNotes: Record split required between the historical national NARO portfolio and the later Fukuoka Prefecture portfolio. The exact researcher is resolved through name, crop specialty, publications, and employer chronology.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Caihong Zhong is a professor and kiwifruit breeding team leader at Wuhan Botanical Garden, Chinese Academy of Sciences. Official PVR records identify Wuhan Botanical Garden as the rightsholder for cited cultivars such as Jinyuan. The institute's commercialization company, not Zhong personally, owns the supported Zhongke Mi trademark.
profiles.headquarters: Wuhan Botanical Garden, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Wuhan 430074, China
profiles.leadershipSummary: Zhong leads a sizeable institutional kiwifruit germplasm and breeding program. The official profile and program pages show a continuing team and national germplasm responsibilities, providing a clear institutional bench beyond the individual researcher.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Professor and kiwifruit breeding team leader, Wuhan Botanical Garden, CAS
profiles.trademarkOwner: Wuhan Botanical Garden Horticultural Center Co., Ltd.

webResearchSources.label: Wuhan Botanical Garden official professor profile
Chinese Ministry of Agriculture official PVR grant record for Jinyuan
Wuhan Botanical Garden official commercialization-company and trademark page

profiles.webResearchNotes: Verified public-institution strategic operator. Cultivar PVRs belong to Wuhan Botanical Garden as recorded; the supported trademark belongs to its wholly owned commercialization company, not to Zhong individually.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Hatsuyoshi Kitamura is a Mie Prefecture strawberry breeder. MAFF identifies Mie Prefecture alone as holder of Kaorino, while Yotsuboshi is jointly held by Mie Prefecture, Kagawa Prefecture, NARO, and Chiba Prefecture. The aggregate record must preserve those cultivar-specific ownership sets and must not assign all rights to Kitamura or Mie alone.
profiles.headquarters: Mie Prefecture Agricultural Research Institute, Matsusaka, Mie 515-2316, Japan
profiles.leadershipSummary: A Japanese government adaptation case study identifies Kitamura as principal researcher and manager of the Vegetable Horticulture Section. The Mie institute has a multi-laboratory public team, while Yotsuboshi also has a four-institution breeding and rights bench. A current personal title after the case-study date was not independently verified.
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The government case study supports the institute affiliation and a historical management role, but it does not establish Kitamura's current title.

webResearchSources.label: MAFF official registration for Mie-held Kaorino
MAFF official registration for jointly held Yotsuboshi
Japanese government adaptation case study identifying Kitamura's institute role

profiles.webResearchNotes: Record split required at minimum between Kaorino's sole Mie ownership and Yotsuboshi's four-party joint ownership. This is a public-sector institutional profile, not a private lead.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Nancy B. Lange discovered and patented the Lange Rosy Red Valencia mutation. The patent record shows original individual ownership and a September 2022 assignment by Nancy Lange to ROSY RED. Current patent title should therefore follow that recorded assignee rather than remain attributed personally to Lange, while her official site continues to present her as the grower and commercial program contact.
profiles.headquarters: Woodlake area, Tulare County, California, United States (operating area; no formal corporate headquarters verified)
profiles.leadershipSummary: The public operation remains centered on Lange as breeder, grower, and commercial contact. The official site identifies supporting propagation, orchard, and packing relationships, but no ownership bench, executive team, or formal successor is publicly documented.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Breeder and grower, Rosy Red Valencia
profiles.candidateParentBasis: The patent records a 2022 assignment to ROSY RED and the official cultivar site operates under the Rosy Red name, but the sources reviewed do not establish a full legal entity name or Lange's formal corporate title.

webResearchSources.label: US plant patent and 2022 assignment record for Lange Rosy Red Valencia
Official Rosy Red Valencia biography and operating history
Official Rosy Red Valencia public business contact page

profiles.webResearchNotes: Record split required between the original individual patent ownership and the recorded 2022 assignee. No trademark claim is included because a primary trademark registration/owner record was not verified.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Dr. Mirko Schuster is a senior scientist in sweet- and sour-cherry breeding at Germany's Julius Kühn Institute. Official PBR and patent records for Areko name Schuster as breeder/inventor and JKI as holder/applicant/assignee. The right belongs institutionally to JKI, not personally to Schuster.
profiles.headquarters: JKI Institute for Breeding Research on Fruit Crops, Dresden-Pillnitz, Germany
profiles.leadershipSummary: The institute is led by Prof. Henryk Flachowsky and maintains a century-scale fruit-breeding program. Dr. Susan Schröpfer works on overlapping sweet- and sour-cherry breeding tasks, providing a visible institutional bench alongside the broader JKI team.
profiles.primaryContactTitle: Senior Scientist, JKI Institute for Breeding Research on Fruit Crops

webResearchSources.label: JKI official staff profile for Mirko Schuster
Canadian Food Inspection Agency PBR record for Areko
JKI official Institute for Breeding Research on Fruit Crops page

profiles.webResearchNotes: Verified current federal-institute researcher and institutional rightsholder structure. Public business contact is Schuster's JKI address only; no personal commercial entity or trademark claim was found.
